claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over all positive divisors d of n equals n.
predicate: n >= 1 and sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) == n

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is prime, then the sum of phi(d) over all positive divisors d of n equals n-1.
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (n >= 1 and sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) == n-1)

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a power of 2, then the sum of phi(d) over all positive divisors d of n equals n.
predicate: (not (n > 0 and (n & (n-1)) == 0)) or (n >= 1 and sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) == n)

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a prime power, then the sum of phi(d) over all positive divisors d of n equals n-1 or n.
predicate: (not (is_prime(n) or any(n == p**k for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) for k in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1)))) or (n >= 1 and sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) in [n-1, n])

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is odd, then the sum of phi(d) over all positive divisors d of n equals n if and only if n is a power of an odd prime.

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a product of two distinct primes, then the sum of phi(d) over all positive divisors d of n equals n-2.
predicate: (not any(is_prime(p) and is_prime(q) and n == p*q for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) for q in range(p+1, int(n**0.5)+1))) or (n >= 1 and sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) == n-2)

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over all positive divisors d of n is congruent to n modulo 2.
predicate: n >= 1 and sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) % 2 == n % 2
